page: Amazon Web Services i4g.large is a storage-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration lacks hyperthreading, providing a single thread per core, and includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

A key hardware feature of the i4g.large is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 468 GB NVMe SSD. In terms of performance, the server exhibits average single-core CPU capabilities in stress-ng benchmarks, but falls into the lower performance tiers for multi-core and memory bandwidth tests. Specifically, cached read-only memory bandwidth is measured at 117.39 GB/sec, while uncached read and write bandwidths are 37.47 GB/sec and 18.83 GB/sec, respectively. Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores also reflect weaker multi-core and integer processing capabilities.

From a resource density perspective, the i4g.large offers a high storage-to-compute ratio and a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring local NVMe storage on energy-efficient ARM architecture. However, the trade-off is lower overall compute and memory throughput. This instance is best suited for lightweight database operations, localized caching, log processing, and storage-intensive applications that do not require high-performance multi-core processing.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.large is a storage-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores with single-thread execution, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. A key differentiator is its bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD, providing high-speed local storage capacity. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core compute and memory bandwidth fall into lower percentiles, with uncached read speeds at 37.47 GB/sec. This hardware profile offers a qualitatively cost-effective balance of memory and local NVMe storage for ARM-native applications. The i4g.large is best suited for lightweight database operations, local caching, and storage-intensive workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores without requiring heavy multi-threaded compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.large is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD for localized, storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.large is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 2 Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 468 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An energy-efficient ARM64 instance combining dedicated compute cores with high-speed local NVMe storage and a generous memory footprint.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 2 dedicated physical cores and 16.0 GB of system memory
Includes a bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage
Best suited for lightweight databases, local caching, and storage-intensive ARM workloads
